title: Graph Search, Frontier by Frontier
tagline: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, A* — four search strategies racing on the same grid maze
summary: BFS finds the shortest path on an unweighted graph but explores blindly. Dijkstra handles weights but ignores the goal. A* adds a heuristic and gets you there straight as an arrow — when the heuristic is admissible. Draw walls and weight zones on a grid, watch four classic search algorithms expand their frontiers, and see exactly where each one wastes work.
highlights: Draw walls on a grid by clicking; drag the start or goal; pick BFS / DFS / Dijkstra / A* and watch the frontier expand.
Side by side: same maze, four searches, see which cells each algorithm needlessly explores.
Add high-cost terrain (mud) and watch Dijkstra route around it while BFS plows through.
Toggle the A* heuristic (Manhattan vs Euclidean vs zero) to see the spectrum from Dijkstra to greedy best-first.
Path length, cells expanded, and optimality reported live.
